text: Park County school consolidation fight. Case studies, vote count, talking points, op-ed. Everything for the board lives here.
Quick links
Case studies — four Wyoming counties that already consolidated.
Niobrara County. Closed the Lusk-area elementary back in 2014. Smallest district in the state — worth a hard look.
What happened
Bus runs stretched past an hour each way after the closure. Enrollment kept dropping anyway.
Pull Niobrara enrollment 2012–2020 from the WDE site.
Hot Springs County, Thermopolis. Folded two rural elementaries into town. Bus times went on record.
Findings
Outlying families saw rides grow to 45–50 minutes. Same number we're staring at for Sage Creek.
Get route mileage from the Hot Springs Co. transportation office.
Washakie County, Worland. Tried consolidation, reversed it within two years. Numbers didn't pencil out.
Why it failed
Promised savings never showed. Fuel and driver overtime ate the difference.
Find the board minutes where they voted to reverse.
Big Horn County. Kept the small schools open with shared staff instead of closing them. The middle path.
The arrangement
Shared a principal and specials teachers across two buildings. Doors stayed open, rides stayed short.
Ask Gary whether Park County could share admin the same way.
Board is seven seats. Need four to block consolidation. Updated after every call.
Where we stand
Solid no: me, Caldwell, Morrison. Leaning no: one. Yes: two. Undecided: one.
Call the undecided seat before the November meeting.
Keep it to three points. Bus time, cost, community. Don't get pulled into the weeds.
The three
45-minute rides for first-graders. No savings in four comparable counties. Close the school, you close the town.
Tighten these to one page for the op-ed.
Draft for the Enterprise. About 600 words. Lead with the bus, close with what these schools hold a town together.
Opening
A six-year-old shouldn't spend ninety minutes a day on a bus so a spreadsheet looks better.
Send to Barbara for a read before I submit it.
Constituent meeting at Sage Creek. Reached 85 families across District 3. Good turnout for a weeknight.
Takeaways
Families want the case-study numbers in writing. Several offered to testify at the board meeting.
Send the four county summaries to everyone who signed in.
